# Vendors

Vendor coverage is most useful after the application and device class are already framed. This section is designed to compare portfolio breadth, integration posture, support expectations, and where each vendor tends to fit best.

## Core paths

<div class="portal-links">
	<LinkCard
		title="Vendor portfolio analysis"
		href="/vendors/vendor-portfolio-analysis/"
		description="A framework for understanding how vendor breadth, depth, and support posture affect buying decisions."
	/>
	<LinkCard
		title="Applications"
		href="/applications/"
		description="Use the application to avoid comparing vendors that solve different categories of problem."
	/>
	<LinkCard
		title="Comparisons"
		href="/comparisons/"
		description="Bring vendor analysis into the final shortlist only after device and protocol assumptions are clear."
	/>
</div>

## Vendor review flow

<Steps>
	1. Confirm the hardware category and application boundary first.
	2. Review whether the vendor is strong in that exact category or only adjacent to it.
	3. Compare lifecycle support, regional availability, integration ecosystem, and long-term fit.
	4. Use structured comparisons to turn vendor profiles into a shortlist.
</Steps>
